title: Wall Street Froze. Crypto Didn't
transcript: On Saturday, the 28th of February, at exactly  1:15 a.m. Eastern Standard Time, coordinated   missile strikes began hitting targets across  Iran. Within hours, global panic had set in.   But the world's financial plumbing was completely  frozen. The New York Stock Exchange was shut, the   Treasury Market was dark, and the CME, Commodities  Exchange, wouldn't open for another 40 hours.   The only place on the planet where you could watch  the geopolitical risk of a Middle Eastern war   being priced in real time was a decentralized  crypto exchange. My name is Guy and you're   watching the Coin Bureau. So, are we looking at  the ultimate validation of decentralized finance?   Is the migration of traditional commodities onto  public blockchains the breakthrough we've been   waiting for? Or is this actually a terrifying  glimpse into a hyper financialized future where   the market never sleeps and retail traders are the  ultimate exit liquidity? Well, that's exactly what   we're going to cover today. First things first  though, let's look at the sheer scale of the   financial blackout that occurred during the final  moments of February. When the initial wave of US   and Israeli strikes hit Iran, the traditional  financial system was entirely paralyzed.   Investors holding stocks, bonds, and traditional  commodities had absolutely no exit liquidity.   They were forced to sit on their hands and wait  for the Monday morning gap down. But the crypto   market didn't have that sort of time cushion to  absorb the macroeconomic shock. Bitcoin, which   had been trading near $65,000 just prior to the  news, immediately plummeted to roughly $63,000.   That initial drop wiped out over $300 million  in leverage long positions across centralized   exchanges in a matter of hours. And by the time  traditional markets finally opened on Monday,   the S&P 500 had cut its losses after falling 1.2%  at its lows. And the VIX volatility index was on   its way to surging 24% to approach the critical  30point panic threshold. But here is where the   story gets genuinely fascinating because price  discovery didn't actually stop over the weekend.   It just migrated entirely to decentralized  exchanges. While Wall Street was offline,   a decentralized perpetuals exchange called  Hyperlquid became the de facto global pricing   mechanism for real world commodities. Here's how  it works. Hyperlquid uses a framework called HIP   3 which allows for the permissionless listing of  traditional financial assets. These are perpetual   futures contracts that track the price of an asset  like oil or gold, but they never expire and they   settle instantly in USDC stable coins. During  the weekend strikes, the oil linked perpetual   contracts on Hyperlid jumped more than 5% to $7126  per barrel. Silver perpetuals saw an astonishing   spike in activity, logging over $1.1 billion in a  single 24hour trading period. And gold contracts   saw roughly $173 million in weekend volume, while  the traditional Comx exchange sat completely dark.   traders were effectively pricing in the  geopolitical risk of the Iran war before   traditional brokers even unlocked their doors.  It proved that 24/7 blockchain infrastructure   is fundamentally superior to legacy banking hours.  And speaking of tracking these macro moves before   they hit the mainstream headlines, that's exactly  why you need the Coinb Telegram channel in your   life. It's where we share the latest breaking  news, provide deep dive alpha, and give you   the most important market updates, too. All pinged  directly to your device so you don't miss a thing.   Whether it's a weekend geopolitical shock or  massive ETF flows, our members see it first. So,   sign up today using the link down below or  by scanning the QR code on the screen. Okay,   back to the weekend's action. Now, you might be  thinking that this is a massive victory for crypto   infrastructure, and in many ways, it is. But the  reality is that this 24/7 access comes with a   pretty scary cost. While the crypto market never  closes, its liquidity does not stay constant.   According to research from Amber Data, the crypto  market's liquidity has a distinctive 24-hour   rhythm. Peak liquidity hits around, 1100 UTC  when Asian, European, and American trading desks   overlap. But over the weekend, when institutional  market makers log off, order book depth collapses.   Postc crash data showed order books were roughly  40% below their pre-crash levels. So when a   geopolitical shock hits on a Saturday, the market  is structurally starved of the capital needed   to absorb the panic. And that brings us to the  harder question that the 24/7 trading narrative   is actually masking because thin liquidity is just  the match. The dynamite is cross margin leverage.   When Bitcoin dropped to $63,000, it didn't  just trigger a few stop- losses. It ignited   a cascading liquidation doom loop. You see, in a  cross margin account, your entire portfolio acts   as collateral for all of your open positions.  If one highly leveraged trade goes against you,   the exchange automatically forceells your assets  to cover the margin. That force selling pushes the   price down further, which triggers the next layer  of liquidations, and the cycle repeats itself.   And we saw the brutal reality of this ongoing  cascade on the 8th of March when over $300   million in crypto positions were liquidated in a  single 24-hour window. And according to Coinedia,   91% of those liquidated positions were longs. Over  $270 million in bullish bets were systematically   destroyed because retail traders were  caught offguard by a weekend headline.   Now apply that exact same vulnerability  to the $26.4 billion in tokenized realorld   assets currently sitting on public blockchains.  Black Rockck's Bidd alone has grown to nearly   $2.9 billion in value and it is now being  accepted as collateral on major trading platforms.   When you allow traders to use tokenized US  treasuries or tokenized gold as collateral for   leverage crypto bets, you are directly connecting  traditional safe haven assets to crypto's weekend   liquidation engine. And as BCG estimates that  tokenization will surpass the $7 trillion global   ETF market to reach $16 trillion by 2030, this  weekend volatility is going to bleed directly   into traditional commodities. And that could get  real ugly under the right, or should I say wrong,   conditions. What's the verdict? Well, first off,  the legacy financial systems 9 to5 operating hours   are a relic of the past. The recent geopolitical  crisis definitively proved that when the world   needs immediate price discovery, it turns  to decentralized crypto infrastructure.   The Genie is out of the bottle and traditional  exchanges like the NYSE are already scrambling   to build their own 24/7 tokenized platforms to  catch up. But the notion that this is a purely   positive development for the average investor is  completely overstated. Until deep institutional   liquidity stays active over the weekend,  crypto will continue to act as the world's   financial shock absorber. And that means punishing  overleveraged retail traders every time a headline   breaks on a Saturday. We're witnessing the birth  of a truly global always on market and the growing   pains are going to be absolutely brutal. But  what do you think? Is a 24/7 financial market   the ultimate goal of crypto or does it create too  much dangerous volatility for retail traders? Let   me know in the comments down below. And if you  want to understand how the multi-t trillion dollar   tokenization trend is reshaping Wall Street,  well, you can check out our video on that right   over here. Okay, thank you all for watching and  I'll see you again soon. This is Guy signing off.
summary_hu: A február 28-i iráni rakétatámadások során a hagyományos pénzügyi piacok leálltak, míg a decentralizált kriptovaluta tőzsdék aktívan működtek. A Bitcoin ára azonnal csökkent, több mint 300 millió dollárnyi hosszú pozíciót töröltek. A decentralizált tőzsdék, mint a Hyperlquid, 24/7 kereskedési lehetőséget biztosítanak, amely lehetővé teszi a geopolitikai kockázatok azonnali árazását, azonban a vékony likviditás és az ezáltal keletkező pánik miatt a hétvégék különösen kockázatosak. A tőzsdei piacok leállása bizonyította, hogy a decentralizált infrastruktúra gyorsabb és rugalmasabb válaszokat kínál, de a magas tőkearányú kereskedés folyamatosan veszélyezteti a kiskereskedelmi kereskedőket. A tokentizáció terjedése a kriptotárcák és a hagyományos értékpapírok között feszültségeket gerjeszthet a tőzsdék körében, és valószínű, hogy további volatilitást hoz a piacra.
summary_en: During the missile strikes on Iran on February 28, traditional financial markets froze while decentralized cryptocurrency exchanges operated actively. Bitcoin's price fell immediately, triggering the liquidation of over $300 million in long positions. Decentralized exchanges like Hyperlquid provide 24/7 trading, allowing for the immediate pricing of geopolitical risks; however, thin liquidity and ensuing panic make weekends particularly risky. The shutdown of traditional markets demonstrated that decentralized infrastructure offers quicker and more flexible responses, yet high-leverage trading continuously endangers retail traders. The rise of tokenization bridges cryptocurrencies with traditional securities, which may create tensions among exchanges and likely lead to increased market volatility.
key_points_hu: A hagyományos pénzügyi rendszer leállt, míg a decentralizált tőzsdék működtek.
A likviditás hanyatlása különösen veszélyes a hétvégén, amikor a tőkepiacok zárva tartanak.
A tokentizáció a kripto és hagyományos értékpapírok között feszültségforrást jelenthet.
key_points_en: The traditional financial system froze while decentralized exchanges operated.
Liquidity decline is especially dangerous on weekends when capital markets are closed.
Tokenization could create tensions between crypto and traditional securities.
